zh.lifeSummary: 當Julia Ann Way於1806年6月13日出生在美國紐約州哥倫比亞希爾斯達爾希爾斯達爾時，她的父親Solomon Arad Way24歲，母親Sally Tryon21歲。她於1827年與Russell J Cunningham GG#3在美國紐約州結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1828年11月5日在美國紐約州薩拉托加薩拉托加 斯普林斯享年22歲。

nameMeanings.name: Way
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Wey
meanings: English (Dorset and Somerset): variant of   Way  .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Weij, a topographic name from weide ‘pasture, meadow’ (compare Vanderweide ).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names based on the element wīg ‘battle’ (compare Weyers ). This surname is also found in Belgium.

variants.name: Waye
meanings: English (Devon and Kent): variant of   Way  .

nameMeanings.name: Julia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the old Roman family name   Julius .  A woman called Julia is mentioned in Paul's Epistle to the Romans (Romans 16:15), and the name was borne by numerous early saints. Its frequency increased with the vogue for classical names in the 18th century, and it continues to enjoy considerable popularity, although the recent introduction of   Julie   to the English-speaking world has reduced this somewhat. Well-known bearers include the British actress  Julia Foster  (  b. 1941 ) and American actress  Julia Roberts  (  b. 1967  as  Julie Fiona Roberts ).

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.description: War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

en.lifeSummary: When Julia Ann Way was born on 13 June 1806, in Hillsdale, Hillsdale, Columbia, New York, United States, her father, Solomon Arad Way, was 24 and her mother, Sally Tryon, was 21. She married Russell J Cunningham GG#3 in 1827, in New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 5 November 1828, in Saratoga Springs, Saratoga, New York, United States, at the age of 22.

ja.lifeSummary: Julia Ann Wayが1806年6月13日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州コルンビアヒルスダルヒルスダルで生まれた時，父Solomon Arad Wayは24歳，母Sally Tryonは21歳であった。1827年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州でRussell J Cunningham GG#3と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1828年11月5日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州サラトガサラトガ スプリングズで22歳で死去した。

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Julia Ann Way родилась 13 июня 1806 г. в местности хиллсдеил, хиллсдеил, колумбя, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Solomon Arad Way было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Sally Tryon, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Russell J Cunningham GG#3 в 1827 г. в местности Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 5 ноября 1828 г. в местности саратога спрингс, саратога, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 22 лет.
